texto: Looking with his eyes? Seriously? What else would he look around with?”Trish nonchalantly uses her middle finger to scratch her temple. “Toddlers learn by making connections between the body part and the action.”Vince blinks. “Oh, so talking to a kid like that’s normal?” “Someday I hope to have a conversation where I don’t sound like a character from Star Wars.”“I can see how you’d look forward to that.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Jess Molly Brown: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a amor. O trecho “Looking with his eyes? Seriously? What else would he look around with?”Trish nonchalantly uses he…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amor, comedy-romance, humor pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de humor e motherhood.
